pi_items.source: Direct inspection of task.json shows no 'metadata' field; structure contains id, version, env_id, description, init, hooks, success
Verified in docker-compose.yml ports: 80:80; setup_emoncms.sh EMONCMS_URL='http://localhost' line 8
Verified in setup_emoncms.sh line 284: create_feed('House Temperature', ...)
Verified in setup_emoncms.sh line 284: create_feed(..., tag='temperature', ...)
Verified in setup_emoncms.sh line 284: create_feed(..., unit='degC', ...)
Verified in setup_emoncms.sh line 284: create_feed(..., engine=5, ...). Engine 5 is PHPFina per Emoncms documentation (github.com/emoncms/emoncms)
Verified in setup_task.sh line 18: UPDATE feeds SET interval=10 WHERE id=${FEED_ID}
Verified in setup_emoncms.sh lines 330-331: INTERVAL = 10 # seconds (must match feed creation interval); also confirms 86400 seconds = 1 day
Verified in setup_emoncms.sh line 284: create_feed('House Temperature', ..., interval=60). This is the initial creation value before setup_task.sh resets it to 10.
Same as initial_interval_unit; confirmed in setup_emoncms.sh line 330-331
Emoncms is open-source software maintained at github.com/emoncms/emoncms by OpenEnergyMonitor organization (github.com/openenergymonitor)
Verified in docker-compose.yml line 46: 'image: openenergymonitor/emoncms:latest' (official OpenEnergyMonitor Docker Hub image)

privileged_info_summary: The task involves the real, open-source Emoncms energy monitoring application deployed via official Docker image (openenergymonitor/emoncms:latest). The admin credentials are username 'admin' with password 'admin' to access the application at http://localhost. A feed named 'House Temperature' with unit 'degC' and PHPFina engine is initialized at an interval of 10 seconds by setup_task.sh (reset from the creation default of 60 seconds). The task requires the agent to change this interval from 10 seconds to 60 seconds through the Emoncms UI. The task has a 180-second timeout and maximum 25 steps allowed.
